title: 2026 Global AI Jobs Barometer
note: Primary source. 27-country analysis of 1B+ job postings. Key figures: 7× senior-skill demand in high-AI-exposure junior roles; 52% experienced-hire skills in junior ads; AI-skills wage premium 25% (2024) → 56% (2025) → 62% (2026).

title: Canaries in the Coal Mine
note: Section 1: entry-level hiring in high-AI-exposure roles -13% vs. low-exposure roles. Software developer (22–25) employment -20% from late-2022 peak. ADP payroll data covering one-sixth of U.S. workers. Updated findings reported June 2026.

title: 2025년 상반기 채용시장 분석

author.literal: 대한상공회의소 (Korea Chamber of Commerce and Industry)

note: Section 3 (Korea data): 97.4% of ads required experience; entry-only ads 2.6% of 144,181 total. '중고신입' share 25.7% (2023) → 28.9% (2024).

title: Entry-level work didn't disappear — AI 'seniorized' it instead, PwC finds
note: Section 2: source for '22-year-olds asked to have skills of a 35-year-old' framing.

title: AI isn't killing jobs, it's killing entry-level careers for young workers
note: Secondary source for Stanford Digital Economy Lab findings on junior hiring decline in high-AI-exposure roles.
